methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Vertical Aerospace is a public-stage transportation company that has raised $400M+ to date. The transportation sector comp universe consists of five private peers—Rivian, The Boring Company, Einride, Harbinger, and REGENT Craft—with no public comparables identified. No narrow peer-median valuation method is applicable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage datapoints; the identified peer set lacks the minimum three comparables required for reliable triangulation within tightly filtered cohorts.

Given the constraints of the available dataset, traditional valuation triangulation cannot be responsibly executed. The peer universe is sparse and heterogeneous in stage and subsector within transportation, precluding calibrated revenue multiples, EV/EBITDA analysis, or VC-method returns modeling. A peer-median approach using all five transportation peers would introduce significant noise and mask material differences in technology maturity, go-to-market readiness, and capital efficiency across the group.

This analysis cannot support a specific valuation range without additional datapoints: comparable company financials (revenue, burn rate, unit economics), transaction precedents in aerospace or last-mile mobility, or discounted cash flow inputs. Future valuation work should either expand the comp set to include relevant stage-matched peers across adjacent sectors (logistics, aerospace, automotive) or rely on transaction-level or fundamental-method frameworks using company-specific operational metrics.
